title: Nipah scare in Kerala: Animal Husbandry dept. opens control room for fast intervention
content: Animal Husbandry department has directed livestock owners to immediately inform veterinary authorities if domesticated animals exhibit unusual symptoms or if sudden deaths occur. Control room number is 04952-762050

content: Ms. Natarajan’s counsel apprised the top court that the rejection of her candidature suffered from a ‘patent error’ and warranted judicial intervention

title: India sends measles vaccines, medical supplies to Maldives amid rising cases
content: India has dispatched a consignment of 20,000 doses of measles vaccines and approximately 3 tonnes of medical supplies to support the Maldives, the Ministry of External Affairs said

title: British High Commissioner Lindy Cameron pays tribute to Air India plane crash victims
content: ‘One year ago today, the tragic Air India crash claimed 260 lives, including 52 British nationals,’ British High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron said in a post on X

content: Terming Statehood as ‘the right of Jammu and Kashmir’, Cabinet Minister Sakina Itoo said a protest at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi is also being planned to press for the demand

title: J&K CM raises concern over 15-day closure of Srinagar airport with Defence, Civil Aviation Ministers
content: During the meetings, Omar Abdullah underscores importance of uninterrupted air connectivity to the Union Territory, ‘particularly during peak autumn tourism season when the Kashmir Valley attracts a large number of visitors from across the country’

title: Kerala State Electricity Board eyeing more wind power
content: Power utility secures permission from the State Electricity Regulatory Commission to procure 300 MW of wind power from within the State. It is likely to float tenders for the development of wind power projects soon

title: Dissident Trinamool faction to approach Lok Sabha Speaker, seek recognition as 'real TMC'
content: TMC rebel MP Jagadish Chandra Barma Basunia claimed that 19 members are currently backing the faction

title: Declare Cauvery delta drought-hit, announce relief measures: Anbumani urges T.N. government
content: He warned that weak Southwest monsoon conditions and low storage in Karnataka reservoirs could severely affect Kuruvai cultivation in the delta districts

title: U.S. Navy attacked 3 merchant vessels with Indian seafarers; these strikes must stop: India's firm message to Washington
content: We attach high importance to the welfare and well-being of our seafaring community. When this particular attack on the ship Settebello occurred, we lodged a strong protest with the American side, MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said

title: ‘The 21st century belongs to Narendra Modi’: Naidu marks two years of NDA rule at Tirupati meeting
content: Mr. Naidu said the NDA gave oxygen to a State the YSRCP left on a ventilator, citing a ₹10 lakh crore debt

title: Monsoon 2026 LIVE: Heavy rains lash parts of Himachal Pradesh; wet spell likely till June 17
content: The weather office also issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds with speeds of 30-50 kmph at isolated places in Himachal Pradesh over the next four days

title: Broken promises, rising debt mark two-year rule of coalition government in state, says AP Congress chief Y. S. Sharmila
content: The Congress leader said the Super Six remained partially implemented and accused the government of piling financial burdens on citizens

title: Notices sent to four ex-MLAs for defying AIADMK whip during trust vote in Tamil Nadu Assembly
content: Notices were issued by the Assembly Secretariat to S. Jayakumar, P. Sathyabama, Maragatham Kumaravel, and Esaki Subaya, who were among the 25 rebel AIADMK MLAs who voted in favour of the TVK-led government and later resigned from their posts as legislators and joined the TVK

title: Elephant dies after roaming villages in Thalavadi hills for four days
content: The elephant, estimated to be around 12 years old, had strayed out of the forest area under the Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve and entered villages including Tamilpuram, Arulvadi, and Jeerahalli in search of food and water

title: India summons U.S. Deputy Chief of Mission again after another attack on merchant ship with Indian crew
content: “Yet another vessel with 20 Indian crew on board came under attack. The U.S. envoy was called in by Additional Secretary (Americas) in the Ministry of External Affairs,” sources told The Hindu

title: Hoax bomb threat delays Lucknow-Delhi flight
content: No bomb or any harmful substance had been found in the aircraft during the inspection, the official said, adding further legal and security procedures were underway

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker drops action against 21 AIADMK MLAs after Palaniswami condones them
content: J.C.D. Prabhakar said proceedings had been initiated against four rebel legislators who resigned and joined the TVK

title: Death of BDS student Nithin Raj: Kerala HC reserves order on anticipatory bail plea of accused Dr. Ram
content: Student’s father object to plea on various grounds, including adding charges under Scheduled Caste and Schedule Tribe (Prevention of Atrocities Act), 1989, which is non-bailable, and that there had been a trajectory of events that led to the student’s death

title: Dinesh Trivedi reaches Bangladesh ahead of taking charge as High Commissioner
content: High Commissioner-designate calls for stronger India-Bangladesh cooperation in health, technology and education as he arrives in Dhaka

title: Jairam Ramesh urges Centre to reconsider Great Nicobar airport, backs INS Baaz expansion
content: The Congress leader asked the Minister to reconsider rejecting the full INS Baaz expansion, which he said some senior naval officers had themselves recommended

title: The Indian workers training AI robots to take their jobs
content: Developers think feeding first-person footage, called "egocentric data", into specialised AI models will help robots copy humans

title: Chellanam, a village near Kochi, celebrates Messi with 25-foot cutout ahead of the FIFA World Cup
content: Placing the cutout of Messi at Chellanam has become something of a World Cup tradition in the coastal village near Kochi

title: SIR: Migration, logical discrepancies likely to result in up to 12% voter deletions, claim political groups in Karnataka
content: Within the Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA), Chief Commissioner and District Election Officer M. Maheshwar Rao recently said that 28% of electors could not be mapped during the exercise

title: In Malappuram, Argentina-Brazil fan rivalry is measured in feet, not goals
content: Their passion as well as rivalry has turned flex boards into a contest of its own, with displays stretching from dozens to hundreds of feet and setting new benchmarks in World Cup fandom

title: Royal Family's Daughter-In-Law Shot At As Dispute Escalates In Madhya Pradesh
content: The injured woman, Yogita Singh, is the first wife of Rupendra Kumar Singh, also known as Baba Raja, a member of the Nagod royal family and nephew of Nagod MLA Nagendra Singh.

title: Air India Crash Probe Continues With Diligence, Professionalism: Minister
content: His comments also come against the backdrop of expectations that the Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) may come out with an interim update on the probe into the accident.

title: 'Europe's Weapons Used To Attack India': S Jaishankar On West's Double Standards
content: Jaishankar highlighted Europe's moral ambiguity and blamed them for selling weapons to countries that attack India. He said that India has never done anything to cause harm to Europe.

title: Control Of Hormuz, Release Of Frozen Asset: What Iran Said On Draft US Deal
content: On Thursday, US President Donald Trump said he had called off planned strikes on Iran and claimed a deal to end the war could be signed in the coming days.

title: Calls For Fine-Tuning As Weather Alert Wakes People Up After Midnight
content: Many alerts were sent through the new system on Thursday night, warning lakhs of residents against rain, hail, and high-speed wind. One alert was sent at 1 am.

title: Amazon Founder Calls AI Pessimism Wrong, Blames "Smart" People
content: As AI anxieties spark global job fears, Amazon founder Jeff Bezos claims "smart people" are driving the panic, even as tech giants globally and in India aggressively downsize to build leaner, AI-native workforces.

title: 2 Soldiers Injured In Accidental Grenade Blast Near Line Of Control In J&K
content: Army personnel were conducting routine training and weapon-handling drills when the grenade accidentally detonated, leaving two soldiers seriously injured.

title: Woman Blackmailed Using AI-Edited Obscene Video, 2 Arrested From Karnataka
content: Police said the accused used an AI video application to generate the video of the woman. They then allegedly sent the manipulated video to the victim's mother and demanded money.

title: Inside Swapan Dasgupta's 'Marshall Plan' To Cut Losses, Build Bengal
content: The immediate priority, says Swapan Dasgupta, is to find ways to generate revenue without placing any additional tax burden on ordinary people.

title: Tiger Takes Over Madhya Pradesh's Raisen Fort Hill, Tourist Entry Shut
content: A tiger has been roaming the Raisen Fort hill for nearly 15 days, forcing the Forest Department to restrict public access to the area for the safety of tourists and locals.

title: Yogi Adityanath Flags Off 45 Electric Buses In UP, Inaugurates Noida Depot
content: The buses will run on four key routes in Noida, including Botanical Garden to Greater Noida West, Noida International Airport, Surajpur Collectorate, and New Bus Adda in Ghaziabad.

title: Why Ex-Air India Pilot's Licence Was Restored 15 Years After Suspension
content: The matter stemmed from a 2011 probe into allegations that several pilots had secured licences using forged examination documents.

title: Battle For Steel: Why ArcelorMittal Is In The Eye Of Storm In France
content: For many lawmakers, ArcelorMittal has become a symbol of a fear: Europe is losing industrial foundations on which its economic strength was built.

title: Opinion: Opinion | An Indian-Origin Sikh, A Dead Teenager: The Murder That Has Set Britain On Fire
content: The horrific details came to light last week when a court sentenced the 23-old-old Vickrum Digwa, a British-born man of Indian Sikh heritage, to life imprisonment.

title: Model Arrested For Smuggling Weed Worth Rs 11.82 Crore From Bangkok To Mumbai
content: Harsha Sunny, a former 'Mrs Kerala' 2025 contestant, was produced before the Fort Court which remanded her to judicial custody.

title: US-Iran War Live: Trump Cancels Iran Strikes After 'High-Level Approval' Of Talks
content: US-Israel-Iran War News Live Updates: Trump said key points of a potential agreement have been worked out with support from several countries, raising hopes of a deal soon.

title: "No Setback": Congress' Meenakshi Natarajan After Top Court Rejects Plea
content: The Supreme Court on Friday rejected Meenakshi Natarajan's plea, challenging the rejection of her Rajya Sabha nomination papers.

title: More trouble for Mamata: FIR against ex-Bengal CM over 'inflammatory' speech
content: An FIR has been filed against former West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee for alleged provocative remarks made at a Kolkata rally. The complaint cites concerns over public peace and communal harmony. The TMC has labelled the case politically motivated amidst the party's internal dissent and recent election results, with investigations now underway.

title: 'On my own terms': Kane Williamson retires from international cricket
content: New Zealand's star batsman Kane Williamson has retired from international cricket with immediate effect after a distinguished 16-year career. The batting stalwart, who represented New Zealand in 378 matches across all formats, stated the decision was not sudden and he felt it was the right time to step away on his own terms, expressing optimism for the team's future.

title: Microsoft is not only company 'warning' about using Anthropic Claude Fable
content: Microsoft has restricted Claude Fable 5 from its internal GitHub Copilot tool, citing concerns over Anthropic's new 30-day data retention policy for Mythos-class models. But Microsoft isn't alone. The Information reports AI legal firms are warning the rules could compromise attorney-client privilege, since every other Claude model still supports Zero Data Retention agreements. Enterprises with sensitive workloads are now reconsidering their Fable 5 deployments.

title: Explained: Why Virat Kohli is not playing the first ODI vs Afghanistan in Dharamsala
content: Team India starts its 2027 ODI World Cup journey against Afghanistan. Shubman Gill leads the squad in Dharamsala. Virat Kohli is out with a hamstring injury. Kohli has been in strong ODI form since 2025. India plays 20 ODIs before the World Cup. The team hopes for Kohli's quick recovery for the England tour.

title: 'Gill hasn't really settled down yet': Ex-India cricketer sounds alarm over ODI captaincy
content: Former India opener Aakash Chopra believes Shubman Gill needs to improve his ODI captaincy, despite his batting prowess.  Gill's leadership has seen just two wins in six matches, with two series losses.  The upcoming Afghanistan series offers a crucial chance for the young skipper to establish his authority and build a stronger record, while his exceptional batting form continues.

title: Google donates $50mn to train 300K workers, CEO says infrastructure is key
content: Google is investing $50 million through Google.org to train 300,000 American workers for skilled trade jobs like electricians, welders, and plumbers. This initiative aims to bolster the nation's digital economy by supporting the physical infrastructure built and maintained by these essential workers. The funding will empower various labor unions and trade associations across more than 20 states.

title: Man buys Rs 1.14 lakh AC that wouldn't cool; court orders full refund plus Rs 25K compensation
content: A Delhi consumer court ordered Fujitsu General India to refund Rs 1.14 lakh for a defective AC that failed to cool. The customer faced installation issues and received no satisfactory service despite numerous complaints. The company was also fined Rs 25,000 for compensation and Rs 10,000 for litigation costs, highlighting a clear deficiency in service.

title: Anthropic says sorry to users of the new Claude Fable 5 model
content: Anthropic has revised its safeguard policy for Claude Fable 5 following criticism over undisclosed restrictions. The company will now make refusals and model fallbacks visible to users, apologizing for an "incorrect tradeoff." This change aims to enhance transparency after initial safety measures, intended to prevent misuse in sensitive areas, drew developer concerns.

title: Remembering Jaspal Rana: Indian shooting icon behind Manu Bhaker's Olympic success
content: Indian shooting mourns the loss of Jaspal Rana, a celebrated Asian Games gold medallist and multiple international winner, who passed away at 49. Rana, a prolific shooter and influential coach, shaped generations of talent, notably guiding Manu Bhaker to a historic two-medal haul at the 2024 Paris Olympics. His legacy as a mentor and athlete is profound.

title: Bengaluru child murder case gets murkier: Lawyer, lover visit IVF centre to give egg sample
content: Bengaluru police are exploring a new lead in a six-year-old girl's murder: her mother and her alleged partner were undergoing IVF procedures. The mother, Priyanka, was arrested after a week-long search. Investigators are probing if plans for a new family influenced the tragic events.

title: Sensex ends around 1,700 points up; investors richer by Rs 10.2 lakh cr - top reasons for rise
content: The sharp rally boosted the cumulative market capitalisation of BSE-listed firms by more than Rs 7 lakh crore, taking the overall valuation to nearly Rs 460 lakh crore.

title: 'Harassment': Court on wife pursuing case after Rs 20L alimony against husband
content: The Rajasthan High Court has halted criminal proceedings against a man's elderly parents. The court ruled the ex-wife abused legal processes by pursuing the case after accepting a Rs 20 lakh alimony and consenting to divorce. Justice Dhand emphasized that continuing the trial after a full settlement serves only to harass the family.

title: Retail inflation climbs to 3.93% in May as food prices accelerate
content: Retail inflation climbed to 3.93% in May 2026, up from 3.48% in April, driven by a surge in food prices to 4.78%. While rural areas saw higher overall inflation, urban areas experienced a notable increase in housing costs.  Precious metals and certain vegetables like tomatoes and ginger saw significant price hikes, contrasting with sharp drops in potato and peas prices.

title: MP board class 12th supplementary exam result released: Direct link to download scorecards here
content: The Madhya Pradesh Board of Secondary Education has announced the Class 12 Supplementary Examination Result 2026. Students who appeared for the second examination can now access their scorecards online through the official portals. The result offers a crucial opportunity for candidates seeking to clear pending subjects or improve marks for higher education admissions.

title: Mumbai, Navi Mumbai rental markets diverge as demand-supply trends shift
content: Mumbai's rental market sees demand dip but rents climb, driven by economic activity and a preference for smaller homes. Navi Mumbai, while experiencing a demand drop, offers an affordable alternative with a growing rental benchmark. Infrastructure projects are fueling residential growth across the region.

title: Male corpse joke row: Mumbai's KEM Hospital to take action against Sejal Pawar
content: KEM Hospital is investigating MBBS student Sejal Pawar over alleged disrespectful remarks about male cadavers at a comedy show. An internal committee's findings will guide the hospital's action. The dean deemed the comments unacceptable, while the All India Medical Students' Association condemned the insensitive portrayal of body donors, emphasizing the dignity owed to them.

title: Zorawar light tank faces 2-year delay after Army demands heavier armour
content: India's indigenous Zorawar light tank, crucial for Himalayan warfare, faces a potential two-year delay. The Army's demand for enhanced protection beyond STANAG Level 4 necessitates strengthening armour while maintaining the vital 25-tonne weight limit. This upgrade aims to counter threats and ensure high-altitude mobility, impacting its planned 2027 induction.

title: Is Hrithik finally eyeing Hollywood? Actor's latest international signing has sparks buzz
content: Bollywood star Hrithik Roshan has reportedly signed with Hollywood's renowned Anonymous Content, fueling speculation about his international film debut. This move follows his consistent success in Indian cinema, with acclaimed roles and upcoming projects like 'Krrish 4'. Roshan's venture mirrors a growing trend of Indian actors expanding their global reach.

title: Why Sean Strickland was banned from UFC Freedom 250 at the White House
content: Sean Strickland has finally addressed the growing controversy surrounding his absence from UFC Freedom 250. the historic event scheduled to take place on the grounds of the White House. The UFC middleweight champion took to social media to explain why he believes he was unable to attend the event. His comments came after days of speculation from fans and media members who questioned why one of the promotion’s biggest stars would not be present for such a landmark occasion.

title: SpaceX share listing: Analysts predict $2 trillion valuation for Elon Musk-led space firm
content: SpaceX's impending Wall Street debut is generating significant investor excitement, with shadow and prediction markets valuing the aerospace giant at over $2 trillion. Derivatives and perpetual futures suggest a substantial gain from its IPO valuation, indicating strong pre-trading momentum. A successful listing could reshape investor flows and set a precedent for future mega-IPOs.

title: 'They do it when it suits them': Jaishankar on US tariffs and shifting stance on Russian oil
content: Jaishankar criticized Western nations' double standards on sanctions, particularly regarding India's purchase of Russian oil. He highlighted that the US itself encouraged India to buy Russian crude to stabilize global markets, emphasizing India's decisions are driven by affordability and availability, not geopolitics. Jaishankar pointed out the inconsistent application of sanctions, urging a pragmatic approach.

title: SpaceX IPO: Should Indian investors buy the stock that is set to make Elon Musk a trillionaire?
content: At the IPO price of $135 a share, SpaceX commands a market valuation of around $1.75 trillion, placing it among the most valuable publicly traded companies globally despite not yet turning profitable.

title: Ex-soldier survives bike crash, dies after wife and lover poison saline for Rs 2cr insurance
content: Belagavi police have cracked the suspicious death of ex-serviceman Sandeep Manjaragi, who died in hospital after a minor bike accident. His wife, Suma, and her lover, Pundalik Dombar, allegedly conspired to murder him, fearing his recovery would expose their affair and jeopardize his Rs 2 crore insurance payout. Hospital staff are also suspected of involvement.

title: 'People climbed down using sarees': Moments from Delhi apartment fire that killed mother & 2 kids
content: A devastating fire ripped through a five-storey building in Govindpuri, Delhi, early Friday, claiming three lives and critically injuring two. Trapped residents made desperate escapes using sarees from windows as flames and thick smoke engulfed the structure. The blaze, suspected to be an electrical short circuit, gutted the building and parked two-wheelers.

title: Salman Khan moves to Delhi HC for stay on 'Kala Hiran', teaser dropped
content: Salman Khan has approached the Delhi High Court seeking an urgent stay on the film 'Kala Hiran: Battle for Legacy', alleging infringement of his personality rights. He claims the movie uses a lookalike, his iconic bracelet, and leverages his name for promotion, potentially damaging his image and influencing ongoing legal proceedings related to the 1998 blackbuck case.

title: Mehidy Hasan Miraz rushed to hospital after Bangladesh's series win over Australia
content: Bangladesh captain Mehidy Hasan Miraz was hospitalised for observation after suffering a concussion from a Riley Meredith bouncer during the team's historic ODI series win over Australia. Despite the blow and subsequent discomfort, Miraz bravely continued to play, guiding Bangladesh to a memorable victory with an unbeaten 22.

title: Cricket Ireland issues statement amid Belfast unrest ahead of India T20Is
content: Cricket Ireland is closely monitoring the unrest in Belfast ahead of India's T20I series, scheduled for June 26 and 28. While domestic matches are being reviewed, the board assures that player and fan safety is paramount. The series marks a new chapter for India's T20 side, potentially featuring a historic debut for young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

title: 'Exam window extended to 195 mins': NTA rolls out 'student-friendly' measures for NEET UG 2026
content: NEET (UG)-2026 will feature a longer exam window, now 195 minutes, from 2:00 PM to 5:15 PM. Candidates will receive four rough-work pages, with two placed at the booklet's beginning for easier access, addressing feedback on convenience and accessibility. These changes aim for a fairer, more comfortable exam experience.

title: How US went from barring oil exports to becoming world's top crude seller
content: The US has surged to become the world's top oil exporter, surpassing Saudi Arabia and Russia. This significant shift, driven by robust domestic production and strategic reserve releases, marks a dramatic reversal from past dependence. Disruptions in the Middle East and sanctions on Russia have reshaped global energy dynamics, granting the US new geopolitical leverage.

title: 'Kakoli Ghosh took Rs 5L on camera': Kirti Azad launches explosive attack on rebel TMC MPs
content: "Kakoli Dastidar was caught on camera accepting Rs 5 lakh. Where was her integrity then? Yet today, she questions Mamata Banerjee about bribes. When talking about bribes, she should also reveal how many of the initial eight were bribed... I know all these details. One day, I will expose them. I intend to catch them red-handed the very moment they hand over that Rs 10 lakh. It's not impossible, because the information will come from within their own camp," he said.
